findings.title: Accessibility Compliance Risk
findings.description: The current system has significant accessibility gaps that affect visually impaired and motor-disabled users, which poses a high risk of non-compliance with WCAG 2.1 AA standards. This could lead to legal repercussions and loss of user trust.
assumptions: Accessibility compliance is critical for user base growth.
Legal requirements for accessibility must be met.

evidence.excerpt: Screen reader cannot navigate course catalog or enrollment flow.
Video player lacks captions, transcripts, and keyboard controls.
Accessibility audit found that 60% of text elements fail WCAG AA contrast ratios.
Quiz forms use custom radio buttons and checkboxes that are invisible to screen readers.
Need to conduct a full accessibility audit, create the VPAT.

recommendations: Prioritize accessibility improvements in the MVP.

findings.title: Screen Reader Navigation Fix Dependency
findings.description: Fixing the screen reader navigation issues in the course catalog and enrollment flow is dependent on implementing ARIA roles and semantic HTML elements. This is a foundational change that will impact multiple components.
assumptions: All course catalog components need to be updated for compliance.

evidence.excerpt: The course cards use div-based layouts without ARIA roles.

recommendations: Implement ARIA roles and semantic HTML in the MVP.

findings.title: Video Player Accessibility Enhancements
findings.description: The video player must be updated to include captions, transcripts, and keyboard controls to meet accessibility standards. This is essential for compliance and user satisfaction.

evidence.excerpt: The custom video player has no closed captions, no transcripts, and cannot be operated via keyboard.

recommendations: Replace the current video player with an accessible solution in V1.

findings.title: Color Contrast Improvements
findings.description: Addressing color contrast failures across UI components is necessary to meet accessibility standards. This should be prioritized in the MVP to enhance user experience.
assumptions: Improving color contrast will enhance usability for all users.

evidence.excerpt: Accessibility audit found that 60% of text elements fail WCAG AA contrast ratios.

recommendations: Update color schemes to meet WCAG AA standards in the MVP.

findings.title: Quiz and Assessment Accessibility Fixes
findings.description: The quiz and assessment forms need to be made accessible to assistive technologies by updating custom controls and ensuring error messages are conveyed textually. This is critical for compliance.
assumptions: All assessments must be accessible to all users.

evidence.excerpt: Quiz forms use custom radio buttons and checkboxes that are invisible to screen readers.

recommendations: Revise quiz forms to use standard HTML elements in V1.

findings.title: VPAT Implementation
findings.description: Creating a Voluntary Product Accessibility Template (VPAT) is essential for compliance and to attract government and education sector clients. This should be initiated after the MVP is completed.
assumptions: A VPAT is required for compliance and marketability.

evidence.excerpt: Need to conduct a full accessibility audit, create the VPAT.

recommendations: Conduct a full accessibility audit and create the VPAT in V2.

summary: The analysis highlights critical accessibility gaps in the LearnPath system that require immediate attention to meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards. A phased delivery plan is proposed, prioritizing accessibility fixes in the MVP, followed by enhancements in V1 and compliance documentation in V2. Key dependencies and risks have been identified to ensure a focused approach to remediation.
